name: Aquitanian (Proto-Basque)
notes: Aquitanian (1st–3rd c. CE). Ancestor of Basque, known from ~400 personal names and ~70 theonyms in Latin inscriptions from Roman Aquitania. Connection to Basque CERTAIN: andere = Basque 'lady', seme = 'son', gizon = 'man', hartz = 'bear'. Phonological system matches proto-Basque: 5 vowels, aspiration /h/, two sibilants, two rhotics, rare initial voiceless stops, affricate /ts/. Key evidence for the antiquity and continuity of the Basque language family.

sources.author: Hualde, J.I. & Ortiz de Urbina, J. (eds.)
sources.title: A Grammar of Basque

sources.author: De Hoz, J.
sources.title: Historia lingüística de la Península Ibérica en la Antigüedad, vol. II

sources.author: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I.
sources.title: The Sounds of the World's Languages
